75	def gen(
76	    name: Optional[str] = None,
77	    max_tokens: Optional[int] = None,
78	    min_tokens: Optional[int] = None,
79	    n: Optional[int] = None,
80	    stop: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
81	    stop_token_ids: Optional[List[int]] = None,
82	    stop_regex: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
83	    temperature: Optional[float] = None,
84	    top_p: Optional[float] = None,
85	    top_k: Optional[int] = None,
86	    min_p: Optional[float] = None,
87	    frequency_penalty: Optional[float] = None,
88	    presence_penalty: Optional[float] = None,
89	    ignore_eos: Optional[bool] = None,
90	    return_logprob: Optional[bool] = None,
91	    logprob_start_len: Optional[int] = None,
92	    top_logprobs_num: Optional[int] = None,
93	    return_text_in_logprobs: Optional[bool] = None,
94	    dtype: Optional[Union[type, str]] = None,
95	    choices: Optional[List[str]] = None,
96	    choices_method: Optional[ChoicesSamplingMethod] = None,
97	    regex: Optional[str] = None,
98	    json_schema: Optional[str] = None,
99	):
100	    """Call the model to generate. See the meaning of the arguments in docs/backend/sampling_params.md"""
101	
102	    if choices:
103	        return SglSelect(
104	            name,
105	            choices,
106	            0.0 if temperature is None else temperature,
107	            token_length_normalized if choices_method is None else choices_method,
108	        )
109	
110	    # check regex is valid

115	            raise e
116	
117	    return SglGen(
118	        name,
119	        max_tokens,

136	        dtype,

138	        json_schema,
139	    )

14	"""Sampling parameters for text generation."""
15	
16	import logging
17	from typing import Any, Dict, List, Optional, Union
18	
19	# sre_parse is deprecated in Python 3.11+, use re._parser instead
20	try:
21	    import re._parser as sre_parse
22	except ImportError:
23	    import sre_parse  # Python < 3.11
24	
25	_SAMPLING_EPS = 1e-6
26	TOP_K_ALL = 1 << 30
27	
28	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
29	
30	
31	class SamplingParams:
32	    """
33	    The sampling parameters.
34	
35	    See docs/backend/sampling_params.md or
36	    https://docs.sglang.io/backend/sampling_params.html
37	    for the documentation.
38	    """
39	
40	    def __init__(
41	        self,
42	        max_new_tokens: int = 128,
43	        stop: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
44	        stop_token_ids: Optional[List[int]] = None,
45	        stop_regex: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
46	        temperature: float = 1.0,
47	        top_p: float = 1.0,
48	        top_k: int = -1,
49	        min_p: float = 0.0,
50	        frequency_penalty: float = 0.0,
51	        presence_penalty: float = 0.0,
52	        repetition_penalty: float = 1.0,
53	        min_new_tokens: int = 0,
54	        n: int = 1,
55	        json_schema: Optional[str] = None,
56	        regex: Optional[str] = None,
57	        ebnf: Optional[str] = None,
58	        structural_tag: Optional[str] = None,
59	        ignore_eos: bool = False,
60	        skip_special_tokens: bool = True,
61	        spaces_between_special_tokens: bool = True,
62	        no_stop_trim: bool = False,
63	        custom_params: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,
64	        stream_interval: Optional[int] = None,
65	        logit_bias: Optional[Dict[str, float]] = None,
66	        sampling_seed: Optional[int] = None,
67	    ) -> None:
68	        self.max_new_tokens = max_new_tokens
69	        self.stop_strs = stop
70	        if stop_token_ids:
71	            self.stop_token_ids = set(stop_token_ids)
72	        else:
73	            self.stop_token_ids = None
74	        self.stop_regex_strs = stop_regex
75	        self.temperature = temperature
76	        self.top_p = top_p
77	        self.top_k = top_k
78	        self.min_p = min_p
79	        self.frequency_penalty = frequency_penalty
80	        self.presence_penalty = presence_penalty
81	        self.repetition_penalty = repetition_penalty
82	        self.min_new_tokens = min_new_tokens
83	        self.regex = regex
84	        self.n = n
85	        self.json_schema = json_schema
86	        self.ebnf = ebnf
87	        self.structural_tag = structural_tag
88	        self.ignore_eos = ignore_eos
89	        self.skip_special_tokens = skip_special_tokens
90	        self.spaces_between_special_tokens = spaces_between_special_tokens
91	        self.no_stop_trim = no_stop_trim
92	        self.custom_params = custom_params
93	        self.stream_interval = stream_interval
94	        self.logit_bias = logit_bias
95	        self.sampling_seed = sampling_seed
96	
97	        # Process some special cases
98	        if 0 <= self.temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS:
99	            # top_k = 1 means greedy sampling
100	            self.temperature = 1.0
101	            self.top_k = 1
102	        if self.top_k == -1:
103	            self.top_k = TOP_K_ALL  # whole vocabulary
104	
105	    def verify(self, vocab_size):
106	        if self.temperature < 0.0:
107	            raise ValueError(
108	                f"temperature must be non-negative, got {self.temperature}."
109	            )
110	        if not 0.0 < self.top_p <= 1.0:
111	            raise ValueError(f"top_p must be in (0, 1], got {self.top_p}.")
112	        if not 0.0 <= self.min_p <= 1.0:
113	            raise ValueError(f"min_p must be in [0, 1], got {self.min_p}.")
114	        if self.top_k < 1 or self.top_k == -1:
115	            raise ValueError(
116	                f"top_k must be -1 (disable) or at least 1, got {self.top_k}."
117	            )
118	        if not -2.0 <= self.frequency_penalty <= 2.0:
119	            raise ValueError(
120	                "frequency_penalty must be in [-2, 2], got "
121	                f"{self.frequency_penalty}."
122	            )
123	        if not -2.0 <= self.presence_penalty <= 2.0:
124	            raise ValueError(
125	                "presence_penalty must be in [-2, 2], got " f"{self.presence_penalty}."
126	            )
127	        if not 0.0 <= self.repetition_penalty <= 2.0:
128	            raise ValueError(
129	                "repetition_penalty must be in [0, 2], got "
130	                f"{self.repetition_penalty}."
131	            )
132	        if not 0 <= self.min_new_tokens:
133	            raise ValueError(
134	                f"min_new_tokens must be in [0, max_new_tokens], got "
135	                f"{self.min_new_tokens}."
136	            )
137	        if self.max_new_tokens is not None:
138	            if self.max_new_tokens < 0:
139	                raise ValueError(
140	                    f"max_new_tokens must be at least 0, got {self.max_new_tokens}."
141	                )
142	            if not self.min_new_tokens <= self.max_new_tokens:
143	                raise ValueError(
144	                    f"min_new_tokens must be in [0, max_new_tokens({self.max_new_tokens})], got "
145	                    f"{self.min_new_tokens}."
146	                )
147	        if self.logit_bias is not None:
148	            for token_id in self.logit_bias:
149	                if not 0 <= int(token_id) < vocab_size:
150	                    raise ValueError(
151	                        f"logit_bias must has keys in [0, {vocab_size - 1}], got "
152	                        f"{token_id}."
153	                    )
154	
155	        grammars = [
156	            self.json_schema,
157	            self.regex,
158	            self.ebnf,
159	        ]  # since mutually exclusive, only one can be set
160	        if sum(x is not None for x in grammars) > 1:
161	            raise ValueError("Only one of regex, json_schema, or ebnf can be set.")
162	
163	    def normalize(self, tokenizer):
164	        # Process stop strings
165	        if self.stop_strs is None:
166	            self.stop_strs = []
167	            self.stop_str_max_len = 0
168	        else:
169	            if isinstance(self.stop_strs, str):
170	                self.stop_strs = [self.stop_strs]
171	
172	            stop_str_max_len = 0
173	            for stop_str in self.stop_strs:
174	                if tokenizer is not None:
175	                    stop_str_ids = tokenizer.encode(stop_str, add_special_tokens=False)
176	                    stop_str_max_len = max(stop_str_max_len, len(stop_str_ids))
177	                else:
178	                    stop_str_max_len = max(stop_str_max_len, len(stop_str))
179	            self.stop_str_max_len = stop_str_max_len
180	
181	        # Process stop regex strings
182	        if self.stop_regex_strs is None:
183	            self.stop_regex_strs = []
184	            self.stop_regex_max_len = 0
185	        else:
186	            if isinstance(self.stop_regex_strs, str):
187	                self.stop_regex_strs = [self.stop_regex_strs]
188	
189	            stop_regex_max_len = 0
190	            for stop_regex in self.stop_regex_strs:
191	                stop_regex_max_len = max(
192	                    stop_regex_max_len, get_max_seq_length(stop_regex)
193	                )
194	
195	            self.stop_regex_max_len = stop_regex_max_len
196	
197	
198	# This function gets a strict upperbound on the maximum number of tokens that would need
199	# to be buffered to match the input regex string
200	# NOTE: in the worst case, one character that needs to be buffered corresponds to one

14	"""Constrained decoding with outlines backend."""
15	
16	import json
17	import logging
18	from typing import Dict, List, Optional, Tuple, Union
19	
20	import interegular
21	import torch
22	from outlines.fsm.guide import RegexGuide
23	from outlines.models.transformers import TransformerTokenizer
24	from pydantic import BaseModel
25	
26	from sglang.srt.constrained.base_grammar_backend import (
27	    INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ,
28	    BaseGrammarBackend,
29	    BaseGrammarObject,
30	)
31	from sglang.srt.constrained.outlines_jump_forward import OutlinesJumpForwardMap
32	
33	try:
34	    from outlines.fsm.json_schema import build_regex_from_schema
35	except ImportError:
36	    from outlines_core.fsm.json_schema import build_regex_from_schema
37	
38	
39	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
40	
41	
42	class OutlinesGrammar(BaseGrammarObject):
43	    def __init__(
44	        self,
45	        guide: RegexGuide,
46	        jump_forward_map: Union[OutlinesJumpForwardMap, None],
47	    ) -> None:
48	        super().__init__()
49	        self.guide = guide
50	        self.jump_forward_map = jump_forward_map
51	        self.state = 0
52	
53	    def accept_token(self, token: int):
54	        self.state = self.guide.get_next_state(self.state, token)
55	
56	    def allocate_vocab_mask(
57	        self, vocab_size: int, batch_size: int, device
58	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
59	        return torch.zeros(batch_size, vocab_size, dtype=torch.bool, device=device)
60	
61	    @staticmethod
62	    def move_vocab_mask(vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, device) -> torch.Tensor:
63	        return vocab_mask
64	
65	    def fill_vocab_mask(self, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, idx: int) -> None:
66	        tokens = torch.tensor(
67	            self.guide.get_next_instruction(self.state).tokens, dtype=torch.int64
68	        ).to(vocab_mask.device, non_blocking=True)
69	        vocab_mask = vocab_mask[idx]
70	        vocab_mask.fill_(1)
71	        vocab_mask.scatter_(0, tokens, torch.zeros_like(tokens, dtype=torch.bool))
72	
73	    @staticmethod
74	    def apply_vocab_mask(logits: torch.Tensor, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor):
75	        logits.masked_fill_(vocab_mask, float("-inf"))
76	
77	    def copy(self):
78	        return OutlinesGrammar(self.guide, self.jump_forward_map)
79	
80	    def try_jump_forward(self, tokenizer) -> Optional[Tuple]:
81	        if not self.jump_forward_map:
82	            return None
83	
84	        jump_forward_bytes = self.jump_forward_map.jump_forward_byte(self.state)
85	        if jump_forward_bytes is None or len(jump_forward_bytes) <= 1:
86	            return None
87	
88	        # preprocess the jump forward string
89	        suffix_bytes = []
90	        continuation_range = range(0x80, 0xC0)
91	        cur_state = self.state
92	        while (
93	            len(jump_forward_bytes) and jump_forward_bytes[0][0] in continuation_range
94	        ):
95	            # continuation bytes
96	            byte_edge = jump_forward_bytes.pop(0)
97	            suffix_bytes.append(byte_edge[0])
98	            cur_state = byte_edge[1]
99	
100	        suffix_tokens = [f"<0x{hex(b)[2:].upper()}>" for b in suffix_bytes]
101	        suffix_ids = tokenizer.convert_tokens_to_ids(suffix_tokens)
102	        return suffix_ids, cur_state
103	
104	    def jump_forward_str_state(self, helper: Tuple[List[int], str]) -> Tuple[str, int]:
105	        _, cur_state = helper
106	        return self.jump_forward_map.jump_forward_symbol(cur_state)
107	
108	    def jump_and_retokenize(
109	        self, old_output_ids: List[int], new_output_ids: List[int], next_state: int
110	    ):
111	        self.state = next_state

114	class OutlinesGrammarBackend(BaseGrammarBackend):
115	    def __init__(
116	        self,
117	        tokenizer,
118	        whitespace_pattern: str | None,
119	    ):
120	        super().__init__()
121	
122	        try:
123	            self.outlines_tokenizer = TransformerTokenizer(tokenizer)
124	        except AttributeError:
125	            # FIXME: tmp fix for chatglm2 & chatglm3 (pad_token_id=0)
126	            origin_pad_token_id = tokenizer.pad_token_id
127	
128	            def fset(self, value):
129	                self._value = value
130	
131	            type(tokenizer).pad_token_id = property(
132	                fget=type(tokenizer).pad_token_id.fget, fset=fset
133	            )
134	            self.outlines_tokenizer = TransformerTokenizer(tokenizer)
135	            self.outlines_tokenizer.tokenizer.pad_token_id = origin_pad_token_id
136	            self.outlines_tokenizer.pad_token_id = origin_pad_token_id
137	            self.outlines_tokenizer.pad_token = (
138	                self.outlines_tokenizer.tokenizer.pad_token
139	            )
140	            self.outlines_tokenizer.vocabulary = (
141	                self.outlines_tokenizer.tokenizer.get_vocab()
142	            )
143	        self.whitespace_pattern = whitespace_pattern
144	
145	    def _compile_regex(self, regex: str) -> Optional[OutlinesGrammar]:
146	        try:
147	            if hasattr(RegexGuide, "from_regex"):
148	                # outlines >= 0.1.1
149	                guide = RegexGuide.from_regex(regex, self.outlines_tokenizer)
150	            else:
151	                # outlines <= 0.0.46
152	                guide = RegexGuide(regex, self.outlines_tokenizer)
153	        except interegular.patterns.InvalidSyntax as e:
154	            logger.error(f"Hit invalid regex schema: {regex=}, {e=}")
155	            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
156	
157	        jump_forward_map = None
158	        return OutlinesGrammar(guide, jump_forward_map)
159	
160	    def dispatch_ebnf(self, key_string: str):
161	        return super().dispatch_ebnf(key_string)
162	
163	    def dispatch_structural_tag(self, key_string: str):
164	        return super().dispatch_structural_tag(key_string)
165	
166	    def dispatch_json(self, key_string: str):
167	        try:
168	            regex = build_regex_from_object(
169	                key_string,
170	                whitespace_pattern=self.whitespace_pattern,
171	            )
172	        except (NotImplementedError, json.decoder.JSONDecodeError, ValueError) as e:
173	            logger.error(f"Hit invalid json_schema: {key_string=}, {e=}")
174	            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
175	        return self._compile_regex(regex)
176	
177	    def dispatch_regex(self, key_string: str):
178	        return self._compile_regex(key_string)
179	
180	
181	def build_regex_from_object(
182	    object: Union[str, BaseModel, Dict], whitespace_pattern: Optional[str] = None
183	):
184	    if isinstance(object, type(BaseModel)):
185	        schema = json.dumps(object.model_json_schema())
186	    elif isinstance(object, Dict):
187	        schema = json.dumps(object)
188	    else:
189	        schema = object
190	    return build_regex_from_schema(schema, whitespace_pattern)

24	class GrammarManager:
25	    def __init__(self, scheduler: Scheduler):
26	        self.scheduler = scheduler
27	        self.server_args = scheduler.server_args
28	        self.grammar_queue: List[Req] = []
29	        if not self.server_args.skip_tokenizer_init:
30	            self.grammar_backend = create_grammar_backend(
31	                self.server_args,
32	                scheduler.tokenizer,
33	                scheduler.model_config.vocab_size,
34	                scheduler.model_config.hf_eos_token_id,
35	            )
36	        else:
37	            self.grammar_backend = None
38	
39	        self.grammar_sync_group = scheduler.dp_tp_cpu_group
40	        self.grammar_sync_size = scheduler.dp_tp_group.world_size
41	        self.grammar_sync_entry = scheduler.dp_tp_group.first_rank
42	        self.is_grammar_sync_entry = scheduler.dp_tp_group.is_first_rank
43	
44	        self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL = envs.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL.get()
45	        self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_MAX_POLL_ITERATIONS = (
46	            envs.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_MAX_POLL_ITERATIONS.get()
47	        )
48	
49	    def __len__(self):
50	        return len(self.grammar_queue)
51	
52	    def clear(self):
53	        if self.grammar_backend:
54	            self.grammar_backend.reset()
55	
56	    def has_waiting_grammars(self) -> bool:
57	        return len(self.grammar_queue) > 0
58	
59	    def abort_requests(self, recv_req: AbortReq):
60	        for req in self.grammar_queue:
61	            if recv_req.abort_all or req.rid.startswith(recv_req.rid):
62	                logger.debug(f"Abort grammar queue request. {req.rid=}")
63	                if req.grammar:
64	                    req.grammar.cancel()
65	                req.set_finish_with_abort("Aborted by AbortReq.")
66	
67	    def process_req_with_grammar(self, req: Req) -> bool:
68	        # Init grammar cache for this request
69	        add_to_grammar_queue = False
70	        if (
71	            req.sampling_params.json_schema is not None
72	            or req.sampling_params.regex is not None
73	            or req.sampling_params.ebnf is not None
74	            or req.sampling_params.structural_tag is not None
75	        ):
76	            if self.grammar_backend is None:
77	                error_msg = "Grammar-based generation (json_schema, regex, ebnf, structural_tag) is not supported when the server is launched with --grammar-backend none"
78	                req.set_finish_with_abort(error_msg)
79	            else:
80	                if req.sampling_params.json_schema is not None:
81	                    key = ("json", req.sampling_params.json_schema)
82	                elif req.sampling_params.regex is not None:
83	                    key = ("regex", req.sampling_params.regex)
84	                elif req.sampling_params.ebnf is not None:
85	                    key = ("ebnf", req.sampling_params.ebnf)
86	                elif req.sampling_params.structural_tag:
87	                    key = ("structural_tag", req.sampling_params.structural_tag)
88	
89	                value, cache_hit = self.grammar_backend.get_cached_or_future_value(
90	                    key, req.require_reasoning
91	                )
92	                req.grammar = value
93	
94	                if not cache_hit:
95	                    req.grammar_key = key
96	                    add_to_grammar_queue = True
97	                else:
98	                    if value is INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ:  # We hit a cached invalid grammar.
99	                        error_msg = f"Invalid grammar request with cache hit: {key=}"
100	                        req.set_finish_with_abort(error_msg)
101	
102	        if add_to_grammar_queue:
103	            self.grammar_queue.append(req)
104	
105	        return add_to_grammar_queue
106	
107	    def get_ready_grammar_requests(self) -> List[Req]:
108	        """
109	        Move requests whose grammar objects are ready from grammar_queue to waiting_queue.
110	
111	        Rank i returns two sets ready_reqs_i, failed_reqs_i
112	        ready_reqs_all = all_gather(ready_reqs_i)
113	        failed_reqs_all = all_gather(failed_reqs_i)
114	
115	        ready_reqs = intersect(ready_reqs_all)
116	        failed_reqs = union(failed_reqs_all)
117	        """
118	        ready_req_idxs: set[int] = set()
119	        failed_req_idxs: set[int] = set()
120	
121	        # Poll for ready requests
122	        start_time = time.perf_counter()
123	        while time.perf_counter() - start_time < self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL:
124	            for i, req in enumerate(self.grammar_queue):
125	                if i in ready_req_idxs:
126	                    continue
127	
128	                if req.finished() or req.grammar is None:  # It is aborted by AbortReq
129	                    ready_req_idxs.add(i)
130	                    continue
131	
132	                assert isinstance(req.grammar, futures.Future), f"{req=}"
133	                if req.grammar.done():
134	                    ready_req_idxs.add(i)
135	
136	            # Sleep a bit to avoid busy waiting
137	            time.sleep(self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL / 10)
138	
139	        # Check failed requests
140	        for i, req in enumerate(self.grammar_queue):
141	            if i not in ready_req_idxs:
142	                self.grammar_queue[i].grammar_wait_ct += 1
143	                if (
144	                    self.grammar_queue[i].grammar_wait_ct
145	                    >= self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_MAX_POLL_ITERATIONS
146	                ):
147	                    # Timeout after max poll iterations
148	                    # The actual waiting time is SGLANG_GRAMMAR_MAX_POLL_ITERATIONS * max(SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL, GPU_forward_batch_latency)
149	                    failed_req_idxs.add(i)
150	
151	        # Sync ready and failed requests across all ranks
152	        if self.grammar_sync_size == 1:
153	            synced_ready_req_idxs = ready_req_idxs
154	            synced_failed_req_idxs = failed_req_idxs
155	        else:
156	            all_gather_output = [None] * self.grammar_sync_size
157	            torch.distributed.all_gather_object(
158	                all_gather_output,
159	                (ready_req_idxs, failed_req_idxs),
160	                group=self.grammar_sync_group,
161	            )
162	            synced_ready_req_idxs = set.intersection(*[x[0] for x in all_gather_output])
163	            synced_failed_req_idxs = set.union(*[x[1] for x in all_gather_output])
164	
165	        # Return ready requests
166	        return_reqs: List[Req] = []
167	        for i in synced_ready_req_idxs:
168	            req = self.grammar_queue[i]
169	            return_reqs.append(req)
170	            if req.finished() or req.grammar is None:  # It is aborted by AbortReq
171	                continue
172	
173	            req.grammar = req.grammar.result()
174	            self.grammar_backend.set_cache(req.grammar_key, req.grammar.copy())
175	            if req.grammar is INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ:
176	                error_msg = f"Invalid grammar request: {req.grammar_key=}"
177	                req.set_finish_with_abort(error_msg)
178	
179	        # Return failed requests
180	        for i in synced_failed_req_idxs:
181	            req = self.grammar_queue[i]
182	            return_reqs.append(req)
183	
184	            req.grammar.cancel()
185	            self.grammar_backend.set_cache(req.grammar_key, INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ)
186	            error_msg = f"Grammar preprocessing timed out: {req.grammar_key=}"
187	            req.set_finish_with_abort(error_msg)
188	
189	        # Remove finished requests from grammar_queue
190	        self.grammar_queue = [
191	            req
192	            for i, req in enumerate(self.grammar_queue)
193	            if i not in synced_ready_req_idxs and i not in synced_failed_req_idxs
194	        ]
195	        return return_reqs

102	        """

104	
105	        Returns:
106	            A tuple of the jump forward string and the next state of the grammar
107	            (which can be used in `jump_and_retokenize` if needed).
108	        """
109	        raise NotImplementedError()
110	
111	    def jump_and_retokenize(
112	        self, old_output_ids: List[int], new_output_ids: List[int], next_state: int
113	    ) -> None:
114	        """
115	        Jump forward occurs, and update the grammar state if needed.
116	        """
117	        raise NotImplementedError()

120	INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ = BaseGrammarObject()
121	
122	
123	@dataclass
124	class CacheEntry:
125	    value: BaseGrammarObject
126	    event: Event
127	
128	
129	class BaseGrammarBackend:
130	    def __init__(self):
131	        self.executor = ThreadPoolExecutor()
132	        self.cache: Dict[Tuple[str, str], CacheEntry] = {}
133	
134	    def _not_supported(self, key_type: str, key_string: str) -> None:
135	        logger.warning(f"Skip unsupported {key_type=}, {key_string=}")
136	
137	    def dispatch_fallback(
138	        self, key_type: str, key_string: str
139	    ) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
140	        """
141	        This function should not be reached in any case.
142	        """
143	        raise ValueError(f"Invalid key_type: {key_type}={key_string}")
144	
145	    def dispatch_json(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
146	        return self._not_supported("json", key_string)
147	
148	    def dispatch_regex(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
149	        return self._not_supported("regex", key_string)
150	
151	    def dispatch_ebnf(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
152	        return self._not_supported("ebnf", key_string)
153	
154	    def dispatch_structural_tag(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
155	        return self._not_supported("structural_tag", key_string)
156	
157	    def _init_value_dispatch(
158	        self, key: Tuple[str, str], require_reasoning: bool
159	    ) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
160	        s = time.perf_counter()
161	        key_type, key_string = key
162	        if key_type == "json":
163	            grammar = self.dispatch_json(key_string)
164	        elif key_type == "regex":
165	            grammar = self.dispatch_regex(key_string)
166	        elif key_type == "ebnf":
167	            grammar = self.dispatch_ebnf(key_string)
168	        elif key_type == "structural_tag":
169	            grammar = self.dispatch_structural_tag(key_string)
170	        elif key_type == "structural_pattern":
171	            grammar = self.dispatch_structural_pattern(key_string)
172	        elif key_type == "structural_pattern_v2":
173	            grammar = self.dispatch_structural_pattern_v2(key_string)
174	        else:
175	            grammar = self.dispatch_fallback(key_type, key_string)
176	
177	        if grammar is not None and grammar.grammar_stats is not None:
178	            grammar.grammar_stats.compilation_time = time.perf_counter() - s
179	        return grammar
180	
181	    def get_cached_or_future_value(
182	        self, key: Tuple[str, str], require_reasoning: bool
183	    ) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
184	        value = self.cache.get(key)
185	        if value:
186	            copied_value = value.copy()
187	            copied_value.maybe_init_reasoning(require_reasoning)
188	            return copied_value, True
189	        value = self.executor.submit(self._init_value_dispatch, key, require_reasoning)
190	        return value, False
191	
192	    def set_cache(self, key: Tuple[str, str], value: BaseGrammarObject):
193	        self.cache[key] = value
194	
195	    def reset(self):
196	        self.cache.clear()
197

199	GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY = {}
200	
201	
202	def register_grammar_backend(name, init_func):
203	    GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY[name] = init_func
204	
205	
206	def create_grammar_backend(
207	    server_args: ServerArgs,
208	    tokenizer,
209	    vocab_size: int,
210	    eos_token_ids: Optional[set] = None,
211	) -> Optional[BaseGrammarBackend]:
212	    name = server_args.grammar_backend
213	
214	    # Custom grammar backend has the highest priority
215	    if name in GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY:
216	        return GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY[name](
217	            server_args, tokenizer, vocab_size, eos_token_ids
218	        )
219	
220	    # Default grammar backends
221	    if name == "outlines":
222	        from sglang.srt.constrained.outlines_backend import OutlinesGrammarBackend
223	
224	        grammar_backend = OutlinesGrammarBackend(
225	            tokenizer,
226	            whitespace_pattern=server_args.constrained_json_whitespace_pattern,
227	        )
228	    elif name == "xgrammar":
229	        from sglang.srt.constrained.xgrammar_backend import (
230	            TokenizerNotSupportedError,
231	            XGrammarGrammarBackend,
232	        )
233	
234	        # Convert Set[int] to List[int] if needed
235	        eos_list = list(eos_token_ids) if eos_token_ids else None
236	
237	        try:
238	            grammar_backend = XGrammarGrammarBackend(
239	                tokenizer,
240	                vocab_size=vocab_size,
241	                model_eos_token_ids=eos_list,
242	                any_whitespace=not server_args.constrained_json_disable_any_whitespace,
243	            )
244	        except TokenizerNotSupportedError as e:
245	            logger.warning(
246	                f"Grammar backend disabled because tokenizer is not supported by XGrammar: {e}. "
247	                "Falling back to grammar_backend='none'. "
248	                "Structured outputs (JSON schema, regex, EBNF) will not be available."
249	            )
250	            server_args.grammar_backend = "none"
251	            return None
252	    elif name == "llguidance":
253	        from sglang.srt.constrained.llguidance_backend import GuidanceBackend
254	
255	        grammar_backend = GuidanceBackend(
256	            tokenizer=tokenizer,
257	            any_whitespace=not server_args.constrained_json_disable_any_whitespace,
258	            whitespace_pattern=server_args.constrained_json_whitespace_pattern,
259	        )
260	    elif name == "none":
261	        return None
262	    else:
263	        raise ValueError(f"Invalid grammar backend: {name}")
264	
265	    if server_args.reasoning_parser and hasattr(tokenizer, "think_end_id"):
266	        from sglang.srt.constrained.reasoner_grammar_backend import (
267	            ReasonerGrammarBackend,
268	        )
269	
270	        grammar_backend = ReasonerGrammarBackend(
271	            grammar_backend, tokenizer.think_end_id
272	        )
273	
274	    return grammar_backend
275

40	class Sampler(nn.Module):
41	    def __init__(self):
42	        super().__init__()
43	        self.use_nan_detection = get_global_server_args().enable_nan_detection
44	        self.tp_sync_group = get_tp_group().device_group
45	        if is_dp_attention_enabled():
46	            self.tp_sync_group = get_attention_tp_group().device_group
47	
48	        self.rl_on_policy_target = get_global_server_args().rl_on_policy_target
49	        # In RL on-policy mode, deterministic inference is automatically enabled.
50	        self.enable_deterministic = (
51	            get_global_server_args().enable_deterministic_inference
52	        )
53	        # In RL on-policy mode, we use log_softmax to compute logprobs to match the trainer.
54	        self.use_log_softmax_logprob = self.rl_on_policy_target is not None
55	        self.use_ascend_backend = get_global_server_args().sampling_backend == "ascend"
56	
57	    def _preprocess_logits(
58	        self, logits: torch.Tensor, sampling_info: SamplingBatchInfo
59	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
60	        """Apply custom logit processors and handle NaN detection."""
61	        # Apply the custom logit processors if registered in the sampling info
62	        if sampling_info.has_custom_logit_processor:
63	            apply_custom_logit_processor(logits, sampling_info)
64	
65	        # Detect and handle NaN values in logits
66	        if self.use_nan_detection and torch.any(torch.isnan(logits)):
67	            logger.warning("Detected errors during sampling! NaN in the logits.")
68	            logits = torch.where(
69	                torch.isnan(logits), torch.full_like(logits, -1e5), logits
70	            )
71	            if crash_on_warnings():
72	                raise ValueError("Detected errors during sampling! NaN in the logits.")
73	
74	        return logits
75	
76	    def forward(
77	        self,
78	        logits_output: LogitsProcessorOutput,
79	        sampling_info: SamplingBatchInfo,
80	        return_logprob: bool,
81	        top_logprobs_nums: List[int],
82	        token_ids_logprobs: List[List[int]],
83	        positions: torch.Tensor,
84	    ):
85	        """Run a sampler & compute logprobs and update logits_output accordingly.
86	
87	        Args:
88	            logits_output: The logits from the model forward
89	            sampling_info: Metadata for sampling
90	            return_logprob: If set, store the output logprob information to
91	                logits_output
92	            top_logprobs_nums: Number of top lobprobs per sequence in a batch
93	            token_ids_logprobs: Per-sequence list of specific token IDs to retrieve
94	                logprobs for. Each element is a list of token IDs (or None) for one
95	                sequence in the batch. This is used in speculative decoding.
96	            positions: The positions of the tokens in the sequence. Used for deterministic sampling
97	                to get the unique seed for each position.
98	        """
99	        logits = logits_output.next_token_logits
100	
101	        # Preprocess logits (custom processors and NaN handling)
102	        logits = self._preprocess_logits(logits, sampling_info)
103	
104	        if sampling_info.is_all_greedy:
105	            # Use torch.argmax if all requests use greedy sampling
106	            batch_next_token_ids = torch.argmax(logits, -1)
107	            if return_logprob:
108	                original_logprobs = logprobs = torch.nn.functional.log_softmax(
109	                    logits, dim=-1
110	                )
111	        else:
112	            simple_sampling_case = (
113	                not sampling_info.need_top_p_sampling
114	                and not sampling_info.need_top_k_sampling
115	                and not sampling_info.need_min_p_sampling
116	            )
117	
118	            # If requested, cache original logprobs before temperature scaling.
119	            if return_logprob and SGLANG_RETURN_ORIGINAL_LOGPROB:
120	                original_logprobs = torch.log_softmax(logits, dim=-1)
121	
122	            # In RL on-policy mode, we use log_softmax to compute logprobs to match the trainer.
123	            logprobs_via_logsoftmax_kernel = None
124	            if self.rl_on_policy_target is not None:
125	                # TODO: use more inplace ops to save memory
126	                logits_div_temperature = (
127	                    logits.bfloat16().div(sampling_info.temperatures).bfloat16()
128	                )
129	                logprobs_via_logsoftmax_kernel = torch.log_softmax(
130	                    logits_div_temperature, dim=-1
131	                )
132	                del logits_div_temperature
133	
134	            if self.use_ascend_backend:
135	                # Ascend backend: sample from logits directly.
136	                batch_next_token_ids, logprobs = self._forward_ascend_backend(
137	                    logits, sampling_info, simple_sampling_case, return_logprob
138	                )
139	            elif (
140	                self.use_log_softmax_logprob
141	                and self.enable_deterministic
142	                and simple_sampling_case
143	            ):
144	                # RL on-policy path: sample from logprobs to match the trainer.
145	                batch_next_token_ids = self._sample_from_logprobs(
146	                    logprobs_via_logsoftmax_kernel,
147	                    sampling_info,
148	                    positions,
149	                )
150	                if return_logprob and not SGLANG_RETURN_ORIGINAL_LOGPROB:
151	                    logprobs = logprobs_via_logsoftmax_kernel
152	            else:
153	                # Standard path: do softmax and sample from probs.
154	                logits.div_(sampling_info.temperatures)
155	
156	                # In-place op to save memory
157	                logits[:] = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
158	                probs = logits
159	
160	                batch_next_token_ids = self._sample_from_probs(
161	                    probs, sampling_info, positions, simple_sampling_case
162	                )
163	                if return_logprob and not SGLANG_RETURN_ORIGINAL_LOGPROB:
164	                    logprobs = (
165	                        logprobs_via_logsoftmax_kernel
166	                        if logprobs_via_logsoftmax_kernel is not None
167	                        else torch.log(probs)
168	                    )
169	                del probs
170	
171	        # Attach logprobs to logits_output (in-place modification)
172	        if return_logprob:
173	            if SGLANG_RETURN_ORIGINAL_LOGPROB:
174	                logprobs = original_logprobs
175	            self._attach_logprobs_to_output(
176	                logits_output,
177	                logprobs,
178	                top_logprobs_nums,
179	                token_ids_logprobs,
180	                sampling_info,
181	                batch_next_token_ids,
182	            )
183	
184	        self._sync_token_ids_across_tp(batch_next_token_ids, sampling_info)
185	
186	        return batch_next_token_ids
187	
188	    def _sample_from_probs(
189	        self,
190	        probs: torch.Tensor,
191	        sampling_info: SamplingBatchInfo,
192	        positions: torch.Tensor,
193	        simple_sampling_case: bool,
194	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
195	        """Sample from probability distribution (after softmax).
196	
197	        Used for standard sampling with flashinfer/pytorch backends.
198	        Handles both simple (direct multinomial) and complex (top-k/top-p/min-p) cases.
199	        """
200	        if simple_sampling_case:
201	            batch_next_token_ids = sampling_from_probs_torch(
202	                probs,
203	                sampling_seed=sampling_info.sampling_seed,
204	                positions=positions,
205	            )
206	        else:
207	            backend = get_global_server_args().sampling_backend
208	            if backend == "flashinfer":
209	                assert (
210	                    sampling_info.sampling_seed is None
211	                ), "Sampling seed is not supported for flashinfer backend"
212	                if sampling_info.need_min_p_sampling:
213	                    probs = top_k_renorm_prob(probs, sampling_info.top_ks)
214	                    probs = top_p_renorm_prob(probs, sampling_info.top_ps)
215	                    batch_next_token_ids = min_p_sampling_from_probs(
216	                        probs, sampling_info.min_ps
217	                    )
218	                else:
219	                    batch_next_token_ids = top_k_top_p_sampling_from_probs(
220	                        probs.contiguous(),
221	                        sampling_info.top_ks,
222	                        sampling_info.top_ps,
223	                        filter_apply_order="joint",
224	                        check_nan=self.use_nan_detection,
225	                    )
226	            elif backend == "pytorch":
227	                # A slower fallback implementation with torch native operations.
228	                batch_next_token_ids = top_k_top_p_min_p_sampling_from_probs_torch(
229	                    probs,
230	                    sampling_info.top_ks,
231	                    sampling_info.top_ps,
232	                    sampling_info.min_ps,
233	                    sampling_info.need_min_p_sampling,
234	                    sampling_info.sampling_seed,
235	                    positions,
236	                )
237	            else:
238	                raise ValueError(f"Invalid sampling backend: {backend}")
239	        return batch_next_token_ids
240	
241	    def _sample_from_logprobs(
242	        self,
243	        logprobs: torch.Tensor,
244	        sampling_info: SamplingBatchInfo,
245	        positions: torch.Tensor,
246	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
247	        """Sample from log-probabilities using the Gumbel trick.
248	
249	        Used for deterministic sampling with simple cases (no top-k/top-p/min-p).
250	        Requires sampling_seed to be set in sampling_info.
251	        """
252	        assert (
253	            sampling_info.sampling_seed is not None
254	        ), "sampling_seed is required for sampling from logprobs"
255	        sampled_index = multinomial_with_seed(
256	            logprobs, sampling_info.sampling_seed, positions
257	        )
258	        return sampled_index.view(-1).to(torch.int32)
259	
260	    def _sample_from_logits(
261	        self,
262	        logits: torch.Tensor,
263	        sampling_info: SamplingBatchInfo,
264	        simple_sampling_case: bool,
265	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
266	        """Sample from temperature-scaled logits without softmax.
267	
268	        Used for the Ascend NPU backend which handles softmax internally.
269	        """
270	        if simple_sampling_case:
271	            probs = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
272	            batch_next_token_ids = torch.multinomial(probs, num_samples=1).view(-1)
273	            return batch_next_token_ids.to(torch.int32)
274	        else:
275	            assert (
276	                self.use_ascend_backend
277	            ), "Only ascend backend supports sampling from logits"
278	            batch_next_token_ids = top_k_top_p_min_p_sampling_from_logits_ascend(
279	                logits,
280	                sampling_info.top_ks,
281	                sampling_info.top_ps,
282	                sampling_info.min_ps,
283	                sampling_info.need_min_p_sampling,
284	            )
285	            return batch_next_token_ids.to(torch.int32)
286	
287	    def _forward_ascend_backend(
288	        self,
289	        logits: torch.Tensor,
290	        sampling_info: SamplingBatchInfo,
291	        simple_sampling_case: bool,
292	        return_logprob: bool,

294	        """Handle the full Ascend backend sampling path.
295	
296	        Ascend backend has fused kernels that handle softmax internally,
297	        so we sample directly from temperature-scaled logits.
298	
299	        Returns:
300	            A tuple of (batch_next_token_ids, logprobs). logprobs is None

775	    def _resolve_sampling_params(self, sampling_params):
776	        """
777	        Construct sampling param based on default + override values
778	
779	        The default values of sampling are populated in `default_sampling_para` via sgl.function.run(...sampling_args)
780	        , and `sampling_params` contains the override values from sgl.gen().
781	
782	        Here we use default_sampling_para as the base and override the values if they exist in `sampling_params`.
783	        It also extends the stop tokens based on the chat template.
784	        """
785	
786	        # deepcopy is required because the dict has lists inside
787	        clone = copy.deepcopy(self.default_sampling_para)
788	
789	        for item in [
790	            "max_new_tokens",
791	            "min_new_tokens",
792	            "n",
793	            "stop",
794	            "stop_token_ids",
795	            "stop_regex",
796	            "temperature",
797	            "top_p",
798	            "top_k",
799	            "min_p",
800	            "frequency_penalty",
801	            "presence_penalty",
802	            "ignore_eos",
803	            "return_logprob",
804	            "logprob_start_len",
805	            "top_logprobs_num",
806	            "return_text_in_logprobs",
807	            "dtype",
808	            "regex",
809	            "json_schema",
810	        ]:
811	            value = getattr(sampling_params, item, None)
812	            if value is not None:
813	                setattr(clone, item, value)
814	
815	        if self.chat_template.stop_str:
816	            if clone.stop == ():
817	                clone.stop = []
818	            elif isinstance(clone.stop, str):
819	                clone.stop = [clone.stop]
820	            clone.stop += self.chat_template.stop_str
821	
822	        return clone

834	    def _role_common(self, name: str, expr: Optional[SglExpr] = None):
835	        if expr is not None:
836	            role_expr = SglExprList([SglRoleBegin(name), expr, SglRoleEnd(name)])
837	            self.stream_executor.submit(role_expr)
838	            return role_expr

840	
841	            @contextmanager

843	                self.stream_executor.submit(SglRoleBegin(name))
844	                yield
845	                self.stream_executor.submit(SglRoleEnd(name))
846	
847	            return role_scope()

26	TOP_K_ALL = 1 << 30

31	class SamplingParams:
32	    """
33	    The sampling parameters.
34	
35	    See docs/backend/sampling_params.md or
36	    https://docs.sglang.io/backend/sampling_params.html
37	    for the documentation.
38	    """
39	
40	    def __init__(
41	        self,
42	        max_new_tokens: int = 128,
43	        stop: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
44	        stop_token_ids: Optional[List[int]] = None,
45	        stop_regex: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
46	        temperature: float = 1.0,
47	        top_p: float = 1.0,
48	        top_k: int = -1,
49	        min_p: float = 0.0,
50	        frequency_penalty: float = 0.0,
51	        presence_penalty: float = 0.0,
52	        repetition_penalty: float = 1.0,
53	        min_new_tokens: int = 0,
54	        n: int = 1,
55	        json_schema: Optional[str] = None,
56	        regex: Optional[str] = None,
57	        ebnf: Optional[str] = None,
58	        structural_tag: Optional[str] = None,
59	        ignore_eos: bool = False,
60	        skip_special_tokens: bool = True,
61	        spaces_between_special_tokens: bool = True,
62	        no_stop_trim: bool = False,
63	        custom_params: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,
64	        stream_interval: Optional[int] = None,
65	        logit_bias: Optional[Dict[str, float]] = None,
66	        sampling_seed: Optional[int] = None,
67	    ) -> None:
68	        self.max_new_tokens = max_new_tokens
69	        self.stop_strs = stop
70	        if stop_token_ids:
71	            self.stop_token_ids = set(stop_token_ids)
72	        else:
73	            self.stop_token_ids = None
74	        self.stop_regex_strs = stop_regex
75	        self.temperature = temperature
76	        self.top_p = top_p
77	        self.top_k = top_k
78	        self.min_p = min_p
79	        self.frequency_penalty = frequency_penalty
80	        self.presence_penalty = presence_penalty
81	        self.repetition_penalty = repetition_penalty
82	        self.min_new_tokens = min_new_tokens
83	        self.regex = regex
84	        self.n = n
85	        self.json_schema = json_schema
86	        self.ebnf = ebnf
87	        self.structural_tag = structural_tag
88	        self.ignore_eos = ignore_eos
89	        self.skip_special_tokens = skip_special_tokens
90	        self.spaces_between_special_tokens = spaces_between_special_tokens
91	        self.no_stop_trim = no_stop_trim
92	        self.custom_params = custom_params
93	        self.stream_interval = stream_interval
94	        self.logit_bias = logit_bias
95	        self.sampling_seed = sampling_seed
96	
97	        # Process some special cases
98	        if 0 <= self.temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS:
99	            # top_k = 1 means greedy sampling
100	            self.temperature = 1.0
101	            self.top_k = 1
102	        if self.top_k == -1:
103	            self.top_k = TOP_K_ALL  # whole vocabulary
104	
105	    def verify(self, vocab_size):
106	        if self.temperature < 0.0:
107	            raise ValueError(
108	                f"temperature must be non-negative, got {self.temperature}."
109	            )
110	        if not 0.0 < self.top_p <= 1.0:
111	            raise ValueError(f"top_p must be in (0, 1], got {self.top_p}.")
112	        if not 0.0 <= self.min_p <= 1.0:
113	            raise ValueError(f"min_p must be in [0, 1], got {self.min_p}.")
114	        if self.top_k < 1 or self.top_k == -1:
115	            raise ValueError(
116	                f"top_k must be -1 (disable) or at least 1, got {self.top_k}."
117	            )
118	        if not -2.0 <= self.frequency_penalty <= 2.0:
119	            raise ValueError(
120	                "frequency_penalty must be in [-2, 2], got "
121	                f"{self.frequency_penalty}."
122	            )
123	        if not -2.0 <= self.presence_penalty <= 2.0:
124	            raise ValueError(
125	                "presence_penalty must be in [-2, 2], got " f"{self.presence_penalty}."
126	            )
127	        if not 0.0 <= self.repetition_penalty <= 2.0:
128	            raise ValueError(
129	                "repetition_penalty must be in [0, 2], got "
130	                f"{self.repetition_penalty}."
131	            )
132	        if not 0 <= self.min_new_tokens:
133	            raise ValueError(
134	                f"min_new_tokens must be in [0, max_new_tokens], got "
135	                f"{self.min_new_tokens}."
136	            )
137	        if self.max_new_tokens is not None:
138	            if self.max_new_tokens < 0:
139	                raise ValueError(
140	                    f"max_new_tokens must be at least 0, got {self.max_new_tokens}."
141	                )
142	            if not self.min_new_tokens <= self.max_new_tokens:
143	                raise ValueError(
144	                    f"min_new_tokens must be in [0, max_new_tokens({self.max_new_tokens})], got "
145	                    f"{self.min_new_tokens}."
146	                )
147	        if self.logit_bias is not None:

65	@dataclasses.dataclass
66	class LogitsProcessorOutput:
67	    ## Part 1: This part will be assigned in python/sglang/srt/layers/logits_processor.py::LogitsProcessor
68	    # The logits of the next tokens.       shape: [#seq, vocab_size]
69	    # Can be None for certain prefill-only requests (e.g., multi-item scoring) that don't need next token generation
70	    next_token_logits: Optional[torch.Tensor]
71	    # Used by speculative decoding (EAGLE)
72	    # The last hidden layers
73	    hidden_states: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
74	
75	    ## Part 2: This part will be assigned in python/sglang/srt/layers/sampler.py::Sampler
76	    # he log probs of output tokens, if SGLANG_RETURN_ORIGINAL_LOGPROB = True, will get the log probs before applying temperature. If False, will get the log probs before applying temperature.
77	    next_token_logprobs: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
78	    # The logprobs and ids of the top-k tokens in output positions. shape: [#seq, k]
79	    next_token_top_logprobs_val: Optional[List] = None
80	    next_token_top_logprobs_idx: Optional[List] = None
81	    # The logprobs and ids of the requested token ids in output positions. shape: [#seq, n] (n is the number of requested token ids)
82	    # Can contain either lists or GPU tensors (for delayed copy optimization in prefill-only requests)
83	    next_token_token_ids_logprobs_val: Optional[
84	        List[Union[List[float], torch.Tensor]]
85	    ] = None
86	    next_token_token_ids_logprobs_idx: Optional[List] = None
87	
88	    ## Part 3: Prefill-only. This part will be assigned in python/sglang/srt/layers/logits_processor.py::LogitsProcessor
89	    # The logprobs of input tokens.        shape: [#token]
90	    input_token_logprobs: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
91	    # The logprobs and ids of the top-k tokens in input positions.  shape: [#seq, #token, k]
92	    input_top_logprobs_val: Optional[List] = None
93	    input_top_logprobs_idx: Optional[List] = None
94	    # The logprobs and ids of the requested token ids in input positions. shape: [#seq, n] (n is the number of requested token ids)
95	    # Can contain either lists or GPU tensors (for delayed GPU-to-CPU transfer optimization)
96	    input_token_ids_logprobs_val: Optional[List[Union[List[float], torch.Tensor]]] = (
97	        None
98	    )
99	    input_token_ids_logprobs_idx: Optional[List] = None
100	
101	    ## Part 4: Diffusion LLM only.
102	    full_logits: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None

110	@dataclasses.dataclass
111	class LogitsMetadata:
112	    forward_mode: ForwardMode
113	    capture_hidden_mode: CaptureHiddenMode = CaptureHiddenMode.NULL
114	    next_token_logits_buffer: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
115	
116	    extend_return_logprob: bool = False
117	    extend_return_top_logprob: bool = False
118	    extend_token_ids_logprob: bool = False
119	    extend_seq_lens: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
120	    extend_seq_lens_cpu: Optional[List[int]] = None
121	    extend_logprob_start_lens_cpu: Optional[List[int]] = None
122	    extend_logprob_pruned_lens_cpu: Optional[List[int]] = None
123	    top_logprobs_nums: Optional[List[int]] = None
124	    extend_input_logprob_token_ids_gpu: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
125	    token_ids_logprobs: Optional[List[List[int]]] = None
126	
127	    # logits and logprobs post processing
128	    temp_scaled_logprobs: bool = False
129	    temperature: torch.Tensor = None
130	    top_p_normalized_logprobs: bool = False
131	    top_p: torch.Tensor = None
132	
133	    # DP attention metadata. Not needed when DP attention is not used.
134	    # Number of tokens in the request.
135	    global_num_tokens_gpu: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
136	    # The start position of local hidden states.
137	    dp_local_start_pos: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
138	    dp_local_num_tokens: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
139	    global_dp_buffer_len: Optional[int] = None
140	    # Number of tokens to sample per DP rank
141	    global_num_tokens_for_logprob_cpu: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
142	    global_num_tokens_for_logprob_gpu: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
143	    # The gather mode for DP attention
144	    dp_padding_mode: Optional[DpPaddingMode] = None
145	    # for padding
146	    padded_static_len: int = -1
147	
148	    # Whether this batch is prefill-only (no token generation needed)
149	    is_prefill_only: bool = False
150	
151	    mm_input_embeds: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
152	
153	    @classmethod
154	    def from_forward_batch(cls, forward_batch: ForwardBatch):
155	        if (
156	            forward_batch.forward_mode.is_extend()
157	            and forward_batch.return_logprob
158	            and not forward_batch.forward_mode.is_target_verify()
159	        ):
160	            extend_return_top_logprob = any(
161	                x > 0 for x in forward_batch.top_logprobs_nums
162	            )
163	            extend_token_ids_logprob = any(
164	                x is not None for x in forward_batch.token_ids_logprobs
165	            )
166	            extend_return_logprob = False
167	            extend_logprob_pruned_lens_cpu = []
168	            for extend_len, start_len in zip(
169	                forward_batch.extend_seq_lens_cpu,
170	                forward_batch.extend_logprob_start_lens_cpu,
171	            ):
172	                if extend_len - start_len > 0:
173	                    extend_return_logprob = True
174	                extend_logprob_pruned_lens_cpu.append(extend_len - start_len)
175	        else:
176	            extend_return_logprob = extend_return_top_logprob = (
177	                extend_token_ids_logprob
178	            ) = extend_logprob_pruned_lens_cpu = False
179	
180	        return cls(
181	            forward_mode=forward_batch.forward_mode,
182	            capture_hidden_mode=forward_batch.capture_hidden_mode,
183	            next_token_logits_buffer=forward_batch.next_token_logits_buffer,
184	            extend_return_logprob=extend_return_logprob,
185	            extend_return_top_logprob=extend_return_top_logprob,
186	            extend_token_ids_logprob=extend_token_ids_logprob,
187	            extend_seq_lens=forward_batch.extend_seq_lens,
188	            extend_seq_lens_cpu=forward_batch.extend_seq_lens_cpu,
189	            extend_logprob_start_lens_cpu=forward_batch.extend_logprob_start_lens_cpu,
190	            extend_logprob_pruned_lens_cpu=extend_logprob_pruned_lens_cpu,
191	            top_logprobs_nums=forward_batch.top_logprobs_nums,
192	            token_ids_logprobs=forward_batch.token_ids_logprobs,
193	            extend_input_logprob_token_ids_gpu=forward_batch.extend_input_logprob_token_ids_gpu,
194	            padded_static_len=forward_batch.padded_static_len,
195	            is_prefill_only=forward_batch.is_prefill_only,
196	            global_num_tokens_gpu=forward_batch.global_num_tokens_gpu,
197	            dp_local_start_pos=forward_batch.dp_local_start_pos,
198	            dp_local_num_tokens=forward_batch.dp_local_num_tokens,
199	            global_dp_buffer_len=forward_batch.global_dp_buffer_len,
200	            global_num_tokens_for_logprob_cpu=forward_batch.global_num_tokens_for_logprob_cpu,
201	            global_num_tokens_for_logprob_gpu=forward_batch.global_num_tokens_for_logprob_gpu,
202	            dp_padding_mode=DpPaddingMode.SUM_LEN,
203	            mm_input_embeds=forward_batch.mm_input_embeds,
204	        )
205	
206	    def compute_dp_attention_metadata(self):
207	
208	        cumtokens = torch.cumsum(self.global_num_tokens_for_logprob_gpu, dim=0)
209	        dp_rank = get_attention_dp_rank()
210	        if dp_rank == 0:
211	            dp_local_start_pos = torch.zeros_like(
212	                self.global_num_tokens_for_logprob_gpu[0]
213	            )
214	        else:
215	            dp_local_start_pos = cumtokens[dp_rank - 1]
216	
217	        self.dp_local_start_pos = dp_local_start_pos
218	        self.dp_local_num_tokens = self.global_num_tokens_for_logprob_gpu[dp_rank]
219	
220	        hidden_size = get_dp_hidden_size()
221	        dtype = get_dp_dtype()
222	        device = get_dp_device()
223	
224	        if self.global_num_tokens_for_logprob_cpu is not None:
225	            # create a smaller buffer to reduce peak memory usage
226	            self.global_dp_buffer_len = sum(self.global_num_tokens_for_logprob_cpu)
227	        else:
228	            self.global_dp_buffer_len = self.global_dp_buffer_len
229	
230	        self.gathered_buffer = torch.empty(
231	            (
232	                self.global_dp_buffer_len,
233	                hidden_size,
234	            ),
235	            dtype=dtype,
236	            device=device,
237	        )

240	class LogitsProcessor(nn.Module):
241	    def __init__(
242	        self,
243	        config,
244	        skip_all_gather: bool = False,
245	        logit_scale: Optional[float] = None,
246	        return_full_logits: bool = False,
247	    ):
248	        super().__init__()
249	        self.config = config
250	        self.vocab_size = config.vocab_size
251	        self.logit_scale = logit_scale
252	        self.use_attn_tp_group = get_global_server_args().enable_dp_lm_head
253	        self.use_fp32_lm_head = get_global_server_args().enable_fp32_lm_head
254	        if self.use_attn_tp_group:
255	            self.attn_tp_size = get_attention_tp_size()
256	            self.do_tensor_parallel_all_gather = (
257	                not skip_all_gather and self.attn_tp_size > 1
258	            )
259	            self.do_tensor_parallel_all_gather_dp_attn = False
260	        else:
261	            self.do_tensor_parallel_all_gather = (
262	                not skip_all_gather and get_tensor_model_parallel_world_size() > 1
263	            )
264	            self.do_tensor_parallel_all_gather_dp_attn = (
265	                self.do_tensor_parallel_all_gather and get_attention_dp_size() != 1
266	            )
267	        self.final_logit_softcapping = getattr(
268	            self.config, "final_logit_softcapping", None
269	        )
270	        if (
271	            self.final_logit_softcapping is not None
272	            and self.final_logit_softcapping < 0
273	        ):
274	            self.final_logit_softcapping = None
275	
276	        self.return_full_logits = return_full_logits
277	        self.multi_item_delimiter = (
278	            get_global_server_args().multi_item_scoring_delimiter
279	        )
280	
281	        # enable chunked logprobs processing
282	        self.enable_logprobs_chunk = envs.SGLANG_ENABLE_LOGITS_PROCESSER_CHUNK.get()
283	        # chunk size for logprobs processing
284	        self.logprobs_chunk_size = envs.SGLANG_LOGITS_PROCESSER_CHUNK_SIZE.get()
285	
286	    def forward(
287	        self,
288	        input_ids,
289	        hidden_states,
290	        lm_head: VocabParallelEmbedding,
291	        logits_metadata: Union[LogitsMetadata, ForwardBatch],
292	        aux_hidden_states: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
293	        hidden_states_before_norm: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
294	    ) -> LogitsProcessorOutput:
295	        if isinstance(logits_metadata, ForwardBatch):
296	            logits_metadata = LogitsMetadata.from_forward_batch(logits_metadata)
297	
298	        # Multi-item scoring only for prefill-only requests.
299	        if self.multi_item_delimiter is not None and logits_metadata.is_prefill_only:
300	            return self.compute_logprobs_for_multi_item_scoring(
301	                input_ids,
302	                hidden_states,
303	                lm_head,
304	                logits_metadata,
305	                self.multi_item_delimiter,
306	            )
307	
308	        # Diffusion LLM only.
309	        if logits_metadata.forward_mode.is_dllm_extend():
310	            return self._get_dllm_logits(hidden_states, lm_head, logits_metadata)
311	
312	        # Get the last hidden states and last logits for the next token prediction
313	        (
314	            pruned_states,
315	            pruned_states_before_norm,
316	            aux_pruned_states,
317	            sample_indices,
318	            input_logprob_indices,
319	            token_to_seq_idx,
320	        ) = self._get_pruned_states(
321	            hidden_states,
322	            hidden_states_before_norm,
323	            aux_hidden_states,
324	            logits_metadata,
325	        )
326	
327	        hidden_states_to_store = self._get_hidden_states_to_store(
328	            hidden_states,
329	            hidden_states_before_norm,
330	            aux_hidden_states,
331	            pruned_states,
332	            pruned_states_before_norm,
333	            aux_pruned_states,
334	            sample_indices,
335	            logits_metadata,
336	        )
337	        del hidden_states
338	
339	        if not logits_metadata.extend_return_logprob:
340	            # Compute logits for both input and sampled tokens.
341	            logits = self._get_logits(pruned_states, lm_head, logits_metadata)
342	            sampled_logits = (
343	                logits[sample_indices] if sample_indices is not None else logits
344	            )
345	
346	            # Decode mode or extend mode without return_logprob.
347	            return LogitsProcessorOutput(
348	                next_token_logits=sampled_logits,
349	                hidden_states=hidden_states_to_store,
350	                mm_input_embeds=logits_metadata.mm_input_embeds,
351	            )
352	
353	        # Start to process input logprobs
354	        # Normalize the logprob w/o temperature, top-p
355	        self._expand_metadata_for_logprobs(logits_metadata, pruned_states.device)
356	
357	        # Determine whether to use chunked or non-chunked logits processing.
358	        # Skip chunking if:
359	        # 1. Chunking is disabled
360	        # 2. Total count is below chunk size threshold
361	        # 3. DP attention all-gather is enabled (can use "enable_dp_lm_head" to enable chunking)
362	        should_skip_chunking = (
363	            not self.enable_logprobs_chunk
364	            or pruned_states.shape[0] <= self.logprobs_chunk_size
365	            or self.do_tensor_parallel_all_gather_dp_attn
366	        )
367	
368	        if should_skip_chunking:
369	            # Compute logits for both input and sampled tokens.
370	            logits = self._get_logits(pruned_states, lm_head, logits_metadata)
371	            sampled_logits = (
372	                logits[sample_indices] if sample_indices is not None else logits
373	            )
374	            input_logits = logits[input_logprob_indices]
375	            del logits
376	
377	            logprobs_result = self.process_input_logprobs(input_logits, logits_metadata)
378	        else:
379	            logprobs_result, sampled_logits = self.process_input_logprobs_by_chunk(
380	                pruned_states,
381	                sample_indices,
382	                input_logprob_indices,
383	                token_to_seq_idx,
384	                lm_head,
385	                logits_metadata,
386	            )
387	
388	        return LogitsProcessorOutput(
389	            next_token_logits=sampled_logits,
390	            hidden_states=hidden_states_to_store,
391	            input_token_logprobs=logprobs_result.input_token_logprobs,
392	            input_top_logprobs_val=logprobs_result.input_top_logprobs_val,
393	            input_top_logprobs_idx=logprobs_result.input_top_logprobs_idx,

399	    def _get_pruned_states(
400	        self,
401	        hidden_states: torch.Tensor,
402	        hidden_states_before_norm: Optional[torch.Tensor],
403	        aux_hidden_states: Optional[torch.Tensor],
404	        logits_metadata: LogitsMetadata,
405	    ):
406	        pruned_states_before_norm: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
407	        aux_pruned_states = None
408	        token_to_seq_idx = []
409	
410	        if (
411	            logits_metadata.forward_mode.is_decode_or_idle()
412	            or logits_metadata.forward_mode.is_target_verify()
413	            or logits_metadata.forward_mode.is_draft_extend_v2()
414	        ):
415	            pruned_states = hidden_states
416	            pruned_states_before_norm = hidden_states_before_norm
417	            if aux_hidden_states is not None:
418	                aux_pruned_states = [hidden for hidden in aux_hidden_states]
419	            sample_indices = None
420	            input_logprob_indices = None
421	
422	        elif (
423	            logits_metadata.forward_mode.is_extend()
424	            and not logits_metadata.extend_return_logprob
425	        ):
426	            # Prefill without input logprobs.
427	            if logits_metadata.padded_static_len < 0:
428	                last_index = torch.cumsum(logits_metadata.extend_seq_lens, dim=0) - 1
429	            else:
430	                # If padding_static length is 5 and extended_seq_lens is [2, 3],
431	                # then our batch looks like [t00, t01, p, p, p, t10, t11, t12, p, p]
432	                # and this retrieves t01 and t12, which are the valid last tokens
433	                idx = torch.arange(
434	                    len(logits_metadata.extend_seq_lens),
435	                    device=logits_metadata.extend_seq_lens.device,
436	                )
437	                last_index = (
438	                    idx * logits_metadata.padded_static_len
439	                    + logits_metadata.extend_seq_lens
440	                    - 1
441	                )
442	            pruned_states = hidden_states[last_index]
443	            if hidden_states_before_norm is not None:
444	                pruned_states_before_norm = hidden_states_before_norm[last_index]
445	            if aux_hidden_states is not None:
446	                aux_pruned_states = [hidden[last_index] for hidden in aux_hidden_states]
447	            sample_indices = None
448	            input_logprob_indices = None
449	        else:
450	            # Prefill with input logprobs.
451	            # Find 4 different indices.
452	            # 1. pruned_states: hidden states that we want logprobs from.
453	            # 2. sample_indices: Indices that have sampled tokens.
454	            # 3. input_logprob_indices: Indices that have input logprob tokens.
455	            # 4. token_to_seq_idx: map each token to its sequence index
456	            #
457	            # Example
458	            # -------
459	            # Suppose a batch (flattened by sequence):
460	            # [t00, t01, t02, t03, t10, t11, t12, t13, t14, t20, t21, t22, t23, t24, t25]
461	            # extend_seq_lens_cpu           = [4, 5, 6]
462	            # extend_logprob_start_lens_cpu = [0, 5, 3]
463	            #
464	            # Then, the indices are:
465	            # pruned_states         -> [t00, t01, t02, t03, t14, t23, t24, t25]
466	            # sample_indices        -> [3, 4, 7]
467	            # input_logprob_indices -> [0, 1, 2, 3, 5, 6, 7]
468	            # token_to_seq_idx      -> [0, 0, 0, 0, 1, 2, 2, 2]
469	            #
470	            # If chunk is enabled and chunk_size = 3, the chunks will be computed in a chunked manner:
471	            # [t00, t01, t02], [t03, t14, t23], [t24, t25]
472	
473	            sample_index_pt = -1
474	            sample_indices = []
475	            input_logprob_indices_pt = 0
476	            input_logprob_indices = []
477	            pt, pruned_states_list, pruned_states_before_norm_list = 0, [], []

479	            for idx, (extend_logprob_start_len, extend_len) in enumerate(
480	                zip(
481	                    logits_metadata.extend_logprob_start_lens_cpu,
482	                    logits_metadata.extend_seq_lens_cpu,
483	                )
484	            ):
485	                # It can happen in chunked prefill. We still need to sample 1 token,
486	                # But we don't want to include it in input logprob.
487	                if extend_len == extend_logprob_start_len:
488	                    start_len = extend_logprob_start_len - 1
489	                else:
490	                    start_len = extend_logprob_start_len
491	
492	                # We always need at least 1 token to sample because that's required
493	                # by a caller.
494	                assert extend_len > start_len
495	                pruned_states_list.append(

497	                )
498	                if hidden_states_before_norm is not None:

198	# This function gets a strict upperbound on the maximum number of tokens that would need
199	# to be buffered to match the input regex string
200	# NOTE: in the worst case, one character that needs to be buffered corresponds to one
201	# token
202	def get_max_seq_length(regex_str: str):
203	    return _max_length_from_subpattern(sre_parse.parse(regex_str))
204	
205	
206	MAX_LEN = 2**30
207	
208	
209	def _max_length_from_subpattern(subpattern: sre_parse.SubPattern):
210	    total = 0
211	    for token, value in subpattern:
212	        if token in {
213	            sre_parse.LITERAL,  # `value` is any one character
214	            sre_parse.IN,  # Any character within `value`
215	            sre_parse.ANY,  # "."
216	        }:
217	            total += 1
218	        elif token == sre_parse.SUBPATTERN:
219	            # EG: (a\d+) ->
220	            # [(SUBPATTERN,
221	            #   (1, 0, 0, [(LITERAL, 97),
222	            #              (MAX_REPEAT, (1, MAXREPEAT, [(IN, [(CATEGORY, CATEGORY_DIGIT)])]))]))]
223	            _, _, _, inner_subpattern = value
224	            total += _max_length_from_subpattern(inner_subpattern)
225	        elif token == sre_parse.BRANCH:
226	            _, branches = value
227	            total += max(_max_length_from_subpattern(branch) for branch in branches)
228	        elif token in {sre_parse.MAX_REPEAT, sre_parse.MIN_REPEAT}:
229	            _, max_num_repeat, inner_subpattern = value
230	            if max_num_repeat == sre_parse.MAXREPEAT:
231	                total += MAX_LEN
232	            else:
233	                total += max_num_repeat * _max_length_from_subpattern(inner_subpattern)
234	        elif token == sre_parse.AT:
235	            # These are zero-width assertions like ^, $, and \b that don't add to the max
236	            # length
237	            total += 0
238	        else:
239	            logger.warning(f"Got unhandled regex token: {token}")
240	
241	            total += MAX_LEN
242	
243	    return total
244

1986	def _check_all_req_types():
1987	    """A helper function to check all request types are defined in this file."""
1988	    import inspect
1989	    import sys
1990	
1991	    all_classes = inspect.getmembers(sys.modules[__name__], inspect.isclass)
1992	    for class_type in all_classes:
1993	        # check its name
1994	        name = class_type[0]
1995	        is_io_struct = (
1996	            name.endswith("Req") or name.endswith("Input") or name.endswith("Output")
1997	        )
1998	        is_base_req = issubclass(class_type[1], BaseReq) or issubclass(
1999	            class_type[1], BaseBatchReq
2000	        )
2001	        if is_io_struct and not is_base_req:
2002	            raise ValueError(f"{name} is not a subclass of BaseReq or BaseBatchReq.")
2003	        if is_base_req and not is_io_struct:
2004	            raise ValueError(
2005	                f"{name} is a subclass of BaseReq but not follow the naming convention."
2006	            )
2007	
2008

127	    def _handle_dtype_to_regex(self, sampling_params: SglSamplingParams):
128	        if sampling_params.dtype is None:
129	            return
130	
131	        if sampling_params.stop == ():
132	            sampling_params.stop = []
133	
134	        dtype_regex = None
135	        if sampling_params.dtype in ["int", int]:
136	
137	            dtype_regex = REGEX_INT
138	            sampling_params.stop.extend([" ", "\n"])
139	        elif sampling_params.dtype in ["float", float]:
140	
141	            dtype_regex = REGEX_FLOAT
142	            sampling_params.stop.extend([" ", "\n"])
143	        elif sampling_params.dtype in ["str", str]:
144	
145	            dtype_regex = REGEX_STR
146	        elif sampling_params.dtype in ["bool", bool]:
147	
148	            dtype_regex = REGEX_BOOL
149	        else:
150	            raise RuntimeError(f"Invalid dtype: {sampling_params.dtype}")
151	
152	        if dtype_regex is not None and sampling_params.regex is not None:
153	            warnings.warn(
154	                f"Both dtype and regex are set. Only dtype will be used. dtype: {sampling_params.dtype}, regex: {sampling_params.regex}"
155	            )
156	
157	        sampling_params.regex = dtype_regex
158	
159	    def generate(
160	        self,
161	        s: StreamExecutor,
162	        sampling_params: SglSamplingParams,
163	    ):
164	        self._handle_dtype_to_regex(sampling_params)
165	        data = {
166	            "text": s.text_,
167	            "sampling_params": {
168	                "skip_special_tokens": global_config.skip_special_tokens_in_output,
169	                "spaces_between_special_tokens": global_config.spaces_between_special_tokens_in_out,
170	                **sampling_params.to_srt_kwargs(),
171	            },
172	        }
173	
174	        for item in [
175	            "return_logprob",
176	            "logprob_start_len",
177	            "top_logprobs_num",
178	            "return_text_in_logprobs",
179	        ]:
180	            value = getattr(sampling_params, item, None)
181	            if value is not None:
182	                data[item] = value

184	        self._add_images(s, data)
185	
186	        res = http_request(

189	            api_key=self.api_key,
190	            verify=self.verify,
191	        )

210	                **sampling_params.to_srt_kwargs(),

216	            "logprob_start_len",
217	            "top_logprobs_num",
218	            "return_text_in_logprobs",

220	            value = getattr(sampling_params, item, None)

225	        self._add_images(s, data)

227	        res = http_request(

232	            verify=self.verify,

234	        self._assert_success(res)

236

243	                chunk_text = data["text"][pos:]
244	                meta_info = data["meta_info"]
245	                pos += len(chunk_text)
246	                yield chunk_text, meta_info
